ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Conference Hearing
Off Calendar Jun-17-2016 Continued To Nov-18-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Phil Foster and David Wakukawa (by CourtCall) for Jeanne Kwong; Carl Lipperberger for Lau Kwong, Larry Kwong, Amy Kwong and Sarah Kwong; Daniel Bernhard for Jennifer Kwok; Richard Bryan for Feng Ouyang.

The Court noted that he had received and reviewed counsel's joint statement. Mr. Bernhard stated that there is a satisfaction of judgment. He opined that the appeal of the disclaimer trial would affect a variety of issues, but not the life insurance dispute, and he suggested that the parties proceed to trial on that dispute. Mr. Foster stated that payment of the judgment is the biggest issue, with post-judgment interest accruing at the rate of $500,000 per year. He contended that the insurance dispute was related to the other matters subject to the appeal, and could not be resolved independently. He urged mediation and settlement of all issues in a global settlement, asserting that a global settlement would allow structuring of the deal in a way to minimize tax consequences. Mr. Lippenberger said that the parties have mediated for over four days, resulting in the parties getting farther apart rather than closer to a settlement. He asserted that if there were to be any mediation, it should be focused on the life insurance dispute. He agreed that most of the remaining issues were governed by the disclaimer appeal. Mr. Wakukawa stated that he was not repared to agree to mediation. He added that not much discovery has been conducted on the insurance dispute, so that matter is not ready for trial. The Court stated that he would be ordering the parties to appear before Judge Wiss in Department 305 on August 15, 2016 for a mandatory settlement conference. He set a status hearing in Department 204 for November 18, 2016 at 9:00 am in Department 206. He ordered the matter to trial in Department 206 on February 27, 2017 at 9:30 am.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: John Boncato. (Not reported)

MINI MINUTES: The followings were heard: PES-09-292733 Estate of Stanley Chong Kwong & PES-09-293019 In Re: Stanley Chong Kwong.
Appearances: Daniel Bernhard for Jennifer Kwok (in all capacities). Douglas Robbins for Jennifer Kwok & California Financial Mortgage Corp. Carl Lippenberger for Lau Kwong & Larry Kwong (Judgment creditors) & Amy Kwong & Sara Kwong (Beneficiaries in the estate case). Phil Foster for Jeanne Kwong (successor trustee of the Stan Kwong Trust). Richard Bryan for (Cross Defendant) Feng Ouyang. David Wakukawa for Gary Wong (respondent).

Mr. Bernhard reported that there was a 5/7/15 target date, that some parties became unavailable and that there are varying levels of interest in mediation but all are prepared for it. Mr. Foster reported that his client is most motivated for a settlement conference, that there are a number of trials coming up that may not need to happen if they can mediate before 6/29/15. Mr. Wakaukawa stated that the insurance carrier is in New York and would like a representative to appear via telephone. Mr. Bernhard stated that there are a couple of big matters and that global mediation would be a prelude to concluding the smaller matters. Mr. Foster stated that there is no way this can be settled case by case, that they need global mediation and agreed that they need to address the big matters before the smaller can be resolved. Mr. Lippenberger stated that they have discussed available dates, that his client is only available by telephone on those dates and that no one has objected to this. Mr. Bernhard stated that he was not aware that he was only available by telephone and feels this will be an impediment. The Court stated that Judge McBride should define the parameters of who does not. Mr. Bernhard stated that the parties will talk about specific dates this morning, that he will follow up with Judge McBride regarding the available dates and agreed that Judge McBride is in the best position to decide who needs to participate. The Court suggested and the parties agreed to have their first round of mediation with Judge McBride by June 26, 2015. The case management conference was continued to July 31, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Murillo. (Not Reported)

MINI MINUTES: The followings were heard: PES-09-292733 Estate of Stanley Chong Kwong & PES-09-293019 In Re: Stanley Chong Kwong.
Appearances: Daniel Bernhard for Jennifer Kwok (in all capacities). Douglas Robbins for Jennifer Kwok & California Financial Mortgage Corp. Carl Lippenberger for Lau Kwong & Larry Kwong (Judgment creditors) & Amy Kwong & Sara Kwong (Beneficiaries in the estate case). Phil Foster for Jeanne Kwong (successor trustee of the Stan Kwong Trust). Richard Bryan for Feng Ouyang. David Wakukawa for Gary Wong (respondent).

Counsel reported that the civil action is on appeal, that they are anticipating a hearing date at the end of this year, that the remaining matters are the life insurance dispute, the petition for allocation of debt, the disclaimer action, the 850 petition involving two promissory notes, the claim for breach of trust and a second 850 petition regarding the 39th avenue property. The parties agreed to set trial on the disclaimer matter first, set trial on the 39th avenue matter second and stay the remaining matters until after the appeal is decided, but in the interim attempt global settlement before Judge McBride. Trial on the disclaimer was set for June 29, 2015 at 9:30 a.m., in the department of the Presiding Judge room 206 in case number PES-09-293019 (order for the same signed in open court/copies provided to all counsel). Trial on the 850 petition (39th Avenue property) was set for September 28, 2015 at 9:30 a.m., in the department of the Presiding Judge room 206 in case number PES-09-292733 (order for the same signed in open court/copies provided to all counsel). The parties are to select a day in May in which all parties can go before Judge McBride for a mediation session. The parties are also to keep each other informed as to which matters they are required to be present. The case management conferences were continued to May 29, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation on the remaining issues. Judge Cheng informed all parties that he would oversee all matters up until the cases going to trial.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Murillo. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar May-12-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 293019 In Re: Stanley Chong Kwong.

Appearances: Carl Lippenberger for Lau Kwong and Larry Kwong (Creditors). Daniel Bernhard and Eli Coffino-Greenberg for Jennifer Shik-Han Kwok. Paul Tour-Sarkissian for Jeanne Kwong.

The Court suggested that Counsel discuss the matter outside of the Courtroom. Upon returning, Counsel reported that they did not reach an agreement. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the Creditors' Claims being untimely, the validity of the Disclaimer, the Creditors' Claim being mooted by the Disclaimer, the Promissory Notes being in the Corporation Trust and the intent of the Decedent. The Court expressed concern about the notes not being paid. The Court took the Creditors' Claim under submission (orders to be submitted by Mr. Tour-Sarkissian). Mr. Bernhard requested an evidentiary hearing on the 850 petition. The Court suggested Counsel discuss whether the by-pass Trust can be funded by the other assets, talk about what those other assets might be and narrow down the issues. The 850 Petition was continued to June 8, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a status conference.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.
